volume_estimate.methodology: GLP-1 revenue $31.2B ÷ blended avg price $850/pen (US ~$936, ex-US ~$400). Includes Ozempic, Wegovy, Rybelsus (oral adjusted to pen-equivalents). Ex-US pricing significantly lower pulls blended avg down.
volume_estimate.notes: Rybelsus oral tablets converted to pen-equivalents at 1 month supply = 1 pen. Novo sells across 100+ countries at wildly different prices. US is ~55% of revenue but <30% of volume.

companies.volume_note: Guesstimate: $31.2B / ~$500 avg global price per pen. US list ~$935 but global avg lower due to EU/LatAm pricing.

volume_estimate.methodology: GLP-1 revenue $22.6B ÷ blended avg price $970/pen (Mounjaro US ~$1,023, Zepbound US ~$1,060, ex-US lower). Lilly has less ex-US penetration than Novo so blended price is higher.
volume_estimate.notes: Tirzepatide is newer with less ex-US volume. Mounjaro launched in Japan, EU in 2024-25. US represents ~70% of Lilly's incretin revenue.

companies.volume_note: Guesstimate: $22.6B / ~$600 avg global price. Mounjaro US list $1,023 but intl pricing lower.

volume_estimate.methodology: GLP-1 revenue ₹420 Cr ÷ ₹4,200/pen (Obeda price). Management stated target of 12M pens/year at scale. Current run-rate ~1M pens (launched March 2026, annualized from early weeks).
volume_estimate.notes: Just launched in March 2026 so actual volume data limited. 12M pen target is management guidance, not current sales. Revenue figure is projected, not reported.

companies.volume_note: Company stated target: 12 million pens in first year. Obeda pen at ₹4,200.

volume_estimate.methodology: Natco launched at ₹1,290/vial (cheapest in market). No reported GLP-1 revenue yet. Estimate based on being first-mover in vial segment with Eris Lifesciences partnership (Sundae brand at ₹220/shot). Annualized from early March 2026 launch.
volume_estimate.notes: Very early stage — launched same week as patent expiry. Vial format attracts clinic/hospital channel more than retail. Partnership with Eris could accelerate distribution. Revenue will only show in Q1 FY2027 results.

companies.volume_note: Rough estimate based on early market position. No public volume target. Vial at ₹1,290.

entries.summary: Global GLP-1 receptor agonist market estimated at $70.08 billion in 2025, expected to reach $86.93 billion in 2026 at a CAGR of 12.78%, with projections of $201.79 billion by 2033. India's GLP-1 market is valued at USD 110.55 million (2024) growing at 34.3% CAGR through 2030, projected to reach USD 730.8 million by 2033.
entries.content: The latest market sizing data from multiple research firms provides the most comprehensive picture of the global GLP-1 market as of early 2026. Grand View Research estimates the global GLP-1 receptor agonist market at USD 70.08 billion in 2025, set to reach USD 86.93 billion in 2026 growing at 12.78% CAGR to reach USD 201.79 billion by 2033. Eli Lilly's tirzepatide (Mounjaro + Zepbound) emerged as the world's best-selling drug in 2025 with combined revenue of $36.5 billion — outpacing Novo Nordisk's semaglutide portfolio at $33 billion and Merck's Keytruda at $31.7 billion. Lilly's total FY2025 revenue reached $65.2 billion (up from $45 billion in 2024), with 2026 guidance of $80-83 billion. Novo Nordisk guided 2026 revenue to decline 5-13% at constant exchange rates, as IRA pricing, exclusivity losses in India/China/Canada/Brazil, and Catalent integration costs bite. In India specifically, the GLP-1 market was valued at USD 110.55 million in 2024, growing at 34.3% CAGR through 2030, with projections to reach USD 730.8 million by 2033. The Q1 2026 results for both Novo Nordisk (expected May 6) and Eli Lilly (expected late April) are not yet available.

entries.summary: Eli Lilly's FY2025 tirzepatide data confirmed: Mounjaro $23B + Zepbound $13.5B = $36.5B combined, making tirzepatide the world's best-selling drug. Q4 2025 alone: Mounjaro $7.4B (+110% YoY), Zepbound $4.3B (+123% YoY). Q1 2026 results not yet reported; expected late April/early May 2026.
entries.content: Comprehensive review of Eli Lilly's FY2025 tirzepatide performance and 2026 outlook. Full-year 2025: Mounjaro generated approximately $23 billion in revenue while Zepbound contributed $13.5 billion, for a combined tirzepatide franchise of $36.5 billion — representing 56% of Lilly's $65.2 billion total revenue. Q4 2025 specifically: Mounjaro $7.4 billion (up 110% YoY, beat estimate of $6.7 billion), Zepbound $4.3 billion (up 123% YoY, beat estimate of $3.8 billion). At midpoint of 2026 guidance ($80-83 billion), Lilly expects approximately 25% revenue growth. Pricing headwinds are real — the company guided to low-to-mid-teens percentage decline in global pricing driven by lower Medicaid rates, direct-to-consumer Zepbound pricing, and broader policy changes. Two key 2026 catalysts: (1) orforglipron FDA submission for obesity expected in 2026, with GlobalData projecting $11 billion peak sales by 2031; (2) retatrutide regulatory submission also expected in 2026 following TRIUMPH Phase 3 success. Lilly is positioned to lead pharma by total revenue in 2026 for the first time. Q1 2026 earnings not yet reported as of March 30, 2026.

entries.summary: Eli Lilly's Q4 2025 results showed Mounjaro + Zepbound combined $11.7 billion in the quarter alone. Lilly's 2026 guidance of $80-83 billion implies ~25% revenue growth, with US GLP-1 market share at 60.5% vs Novo's 39.1%. Lilly's total FY2025 GLP-1 revenue reached $22.6 billion vs Novo's $31.2 billion.
entries.content: Eli Lilly's Q4 2025 earnings marked a decisive inflection in the GLP-1 leadership race. Mounjaro and Zepbound combined for $11.7 billion in Q4 revenue alone — nearly as much as Novo Nordisk generates from its entire GLP-1 portfolio in a quarter. For the full year, Lilly's incretin portfolio reached approximately $22.6 billion, growing at 96% EPS growth. Lilly's 2026 guidance of $80-83 billion implies ~25% revenue growth, driven primarily by continued GLP-1 volume growth and the anticipated launch of orforglipron (oral GLP-1) in Q2 2026. Critically, Lilly's US GLP-1 market share climbed to 60.5% in Q4 2025, up 2.6 percentage points from the prior quarter, while Novo's fell to 39.1%. Truist Securities estimates Lilly's incretin franchise could reach $101 billion at peak across Mounjaro, Zepbound, and orforglipron. In contrast, Novo Nordisk issued a stark warning alongside its Q4 2025 results: the company expects sales and profit to decline 5-13% in 2026, driven by US price cuts under IRA negotiations (Ozempic, Wegovy, Rybelsus prices effective 2027), exclusivity losses in China, Brazil, Canada, and India, and gross margin compression from the $16.5 billion Catalent facility integration. Novo announced 9,000 position reductions to save DKK 8 billion annually by end-2026.

entries.summary: India's GLP-1 injectable market grew 177% YoY to ₹1,446 crore in MAT February 2026, ahead of the semaglutide patent expiry. Nomura Research upgraded the 5-year market forecast to ₹12,000 crore. The generic wave is expected to add a further ₹5,000+ crore incremental opportunity over the next 12-15 months.
entries.content: The India GLP-1 market's pre-expiry growth trajectory underscores the magnitude of the opportunity. India's injectable GLP-1 segment grew 177% year-on-year to ₹1,446 crore in Moving Annual Turnover (MAT) as of February 2026, before even one generic had launched. This growth was driven by Novo Nordisk's Ozempic and Wegovy (launched June 2025), Eli Lilly's Mounjaro (launched March 2025 via Cipla at ~₹100 crore in early months), and the broader market awareness driven by social media and celebrity usage. Post-patent expiry, Nomura Research upgraded its India semaglutide market forecast to ₹12,000 crore over five years — a 7.5x expansion from the current ₹1,600 crore base. This aligns with Jefferies' estimate that the domestic market could eventually reach $1 billion. Systematix Research is even more bullish on short-term growth, projecting ₹5,000+ crore incremental opportunity within just 12-15 months from generic launches. The key growth driver is affordable access: at ₹1,290-₹1,800/month (vs ₹8,800-₹16,400 for Ozempic), the target population expands dramatically from affluent urban patients to middle-class India. Even a 1% penetration of India's 254 million obese adults would represent 2.5 million patients — a 12x increase from the current 200,000.

entries.summary: Novo Nordisk reported FY2025 results with total GLP-1 franchise revenue of $31.2 billion, up 38% year-over-year. Wegovy sales nearly doubled to $9.8 billion as the company expanded supply capacity.
entries.content: Novo Nordisk reported FY2025 results with total GLP-1 franchise revenue of $31.2 billion, up 38% year-over-year. Wegovy sales nearly doubled to $9.8 billion as the company expanded supply capacity. Management guided for continued growth but acknowledged increasing competitive pressure from generics in emerging markets.

entries.summary: India's GLP-1 market reached an estimated ₹2,400 crore in FY2025, growing 185% year-over-year driven primarily by generic semaglutide launches.
entries.content: India's GLP-1 market reached an estimated ₹2,400 crore in FY2025, growing 185% year-over-year driven primarily by generic semaglutide launches. Analysts project the market could reach ₹5,000 crore by FY2027 as more generics launch and off-label obesity prescribing expands in urban India.

Novo Nordisk's FY2025 annual report revealed Ozempic sales of DKK 127,089 million (up 10% at constant exchange rates), while obesity care products reached DKK 82,347 million (up 31% CER), driven by global Wegovy expansion. The company's GLP-1 diabetes products collectively grew 6% CER to DKK 152,202 million. Novo Nordisk announced a major transformation including 9,000 position reductions targeting DKK 8 billion in annual savings by end of 2026, as gross margin compressed to 81.0% from 84.7% due to acquired Catalent manufacturing site costs. The company accepted IRA negotiated prices for Wegovy, Ozempic, and Rybelsus taking effect in 2027.
entries.summary: Novo Nordisk FY2025: Ozempic at DKK 127B, Obesity care up 31% CER

entries.content: Eli Lilly reported Q4 2025 revenue of $19.3 billion (up 43%), with Mounjaro generating $7.4 billion (up 110%) and Zepbound reaching $4.2 billion (up 122%) in the quarter. For the full year, tirzepatide became the world's best-selling drug, with combined Mounjaro and Zepbound Q3 revenue alone exceeding $10.1 billion. Lilly issued 2026 guidance of $80-83 billion in total revenue, reflecting continued incretin dominance. The CFO noted that approximately two out of every three new prescriptions in the incretin analogue market is a Lilly medicine. Lilly plans to submit oral GLP-1 candidate orforglipron to the FDA for obesity in 2026.
entries.summary: Eli Lilly FY2025: Mounjaro and Zepbound combined revenue surpassed $10B in Q3 alone

entries.content: According to CareEdge Ratings, India's GLP-1 drug market valued at Rs 1,000-1,200 crore in 2025 is expected to grow 5x to Rs 4,500-5,000 crore by 2030, with semaglutide patent expiry as the key catalyst. Jefferies separately estimates the domestic market could reach $1 billion with the right pricing, adoption, and government incentives. Systematix Research projects generic semaglutide could create an incremental opportunity exceeding Rs 5,000 crore in the next 12-15 months. The massive opportunity has drawn over 40 manufacturers preparing 50+ brands, though analysts expect only the top six to eight brands will survive long-term as competition intensifies.
entries.summary: India's GLP-1 market projected to grow 5x to Rs 4,500-5,000 crore by 2030

entries.content: Goldman Sachs Research revised its global anti-obesity drug market forecast down to $95 billion by 2030, from a prior estimate of $130 billion, reflecting trends in pricing erosion, adherence challenges, and patient segmentation. Morgan Stanley maintains a more bullish estimate of $150 billion at peak by 2035. J.P. Morgan projects approximately 25 million Americans will be on GLP-1 treatment by 2030, up from 10 million in 2025. The broader GLP-1 analogues market including diabetes was valued at $54.8 billion in 2024, with BCC Research projecting $268.4 billion by 2030. Patent expiries in India, China, Brazil, and Turkey covering 40% of global population are expected to drive adoption but also accelerate price erosion.
entries.summary: Global anti-obesity drug market forecasts range from $95B to $150B by 2030-2035

entries.content: Novo Nordisk disclosed that barely 200,000 patients are on GLP-1 therapy in India out of an estimated 250 million people living with obesity, highlighting massive untapped market potential as the semaglutide patent expires. The GLP-1 drug market share in India is predicted to expand from 4-5% to 15-20% within 18 months post-patent expiry. Dr. Reddy's is targeting 87 countries for generic semaglutide launches as global patents fall over the coming years. Indian generics firms are positioning not just for domestic sales but to supply emerging markets worldwide, potentially disrupting the $50+ billion global GLP-1 revenue pool over the next decade.
entries.summary: Only 200,000 of India's 250 million obese on GLP-1 therapy as patent expires

entries.content: Novo Nordisk's ADR (NVO) fell to $36.98 by April 3, 2026 — down 43% from its 52-week high of $81.44 — as Eli Lilly's Foundayo (orforglipron) approval on April 1 compounded existing competitive pressures from Indian generics. In direct response to Indian manufacturers pricing generic semaglutide at ₹1,290/month, Novo cut Wegovy and Ozempic India list prices by up to 48% effective April 1, 2026. Bernstein initiated Underperform coverage citing an 'ongoing earnings downgrade cycle' with sparse catalysts. Novo's 2026 full-year guidance remains -5% to -13% at constant FX. The company's market cap declined from ~$640B at peak to under $180B — one of the largest value destructions in pharmaceutical history — as the Foundayo approval directly threatens the oral semaglutide (Wegovy pill) franchise that Novo had positioned as its key growth driver.
entries.summary: Novo Nordisk stock -43% year-on-year; forced to cut India prices 48% as generics and Foundayo approval squeeze revenue
